issues_found: CRITICAL FORMULA-DATA INCONSISTENCY: The paper defines Ea = -ln(c0) where c0 is consistency fraction at k=16, but the actual code computes Ea = 1/c0 where c0 is a fitted saturation parameter from curve_fit(bounds=[0.1, 10.0]). For c0=0.10565 (fitted), 1/c0=9.465 matches data; -ln(0.10565)=2.247 does not. The notation.md Usage Note 3 incorrectly states 'Ea ~9.47 corresponds to c0 ~0.106' under Ea = -ln(c0), which is mathematically impossible. The paper's c0 (consistency fraction) and code's c0 (fitted parameter) are different variables with the same name.

H3 SOURCE DATA MISLABELED AS 'CONFIRMED': analysis_h3.json h3_evaluation.status='CONFIRMED' with note 'Ea is a useful routing signal', but predictor_quality.auc=0.436 and spearman_ea_accuracy=-0.063 (p=0.66) show zero predictive power. The evaluation logic only checks threshold pass (low_ea_accuracy >= 0.75) and ignores AUC/Spearman.
MISSING BIBLIOGRAPHY: Paper cites [1] through [11] but has no References section. Citations cannot be verified. 'Li 2026' and 'ACAR 2026' appear to be future-dated and may not exist as citable works.

TABLE 8 (ERROR CLASSIFICATION) LACKS QUANTITATIVE DATA: Section 6.2 claims 'Execution errors dominate low-Ea failures' but Table 8 only has qualitative descriptions and examples. No counts, proportions, or statistics support this central diagnostic claim.

POST-HOC THRESHOLD = DATA LEAKAGE: The 'optimal threshold' of 9.9999999999 is optimized on the same evaluation data. The 75% figure and 25pp 'irreducible error floor' derived from it are optimistic upper bounds, not measured quantities.

SAMPLE SIZE TOO SMALL FOR ROBUST CONCLUSIONS: n=50 problems is pilot-scale. Only 2 problems at Level 1, 13 at Level 5. No confidence intervals reported for key statistics (Spearman=0.448, AUC=0.436, R2=0.924).

PER-PROBLEM FIT FAILURE UNDERMINES H1 CLAIM: Median R2=0.000 across all models, 80% fit failure, only 8/50 prefer exponential by AICc. Aggregate R2=0.924 may be an averaging artifact, not evidence of a genuine physical law.

NOVELTY CLAIM IS NARROW AND FRAGILE: Novelty rests entirely on H3 negative result. H1/H2 collide with Yang et al. (2025). ACAR (2026) reports conceptually related 'agreement-but-wrong' ceiling (~8pp). Paper does not clearly differentiate consistency-based vs variance-based routing.

SINGLE MODEL LIMITS GENERALIZABILITY: All experiments use Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Instruct on 50 problems. Core claim about consistency-based routing is presented as general but evidence supports only this model.

H5 FALSIFICATION IS UNDER-REPORTED: H5 (consistency-Ea matches saturation-k0) is falsified (Spearman=-0.219, p=0.54, only 10/50 valid k0 pairs) but only briefly mentioned in Section 5.4. This challenges internal consistency of the theoretical framework.

For c0=0.10565 (fitted), 1/c0=9.465 matches data; -ln(0.10565)=2.247 does not. The notation.md Usage Note 3 incorrectly states 'Ea ~9.47 corresponds to c0 ~0.106' under Ea = -ln(c0), which is mathematically impossible. Estimated effort: 30 minutes. Add these CIs to the paper's results sections. Acknowledge n=50 as a limitation prominently. Estimated effort: 45 minutes. Verification: analysis output files contain 'ci_95' fields and paper reports intervals.", "status": "new", "requires_system_change": false, "issue_key": "experiment:at-at-auc-conclusions-confidence-for-for-intervals:ffa66386f305"}, {"description": "PER-PROBLEM FIT FAILURE UNDERMINES H1 CLAIM: Median R2=0.000 across all models, 80% fit failure, only 8/50 prefer exponential by AICc. The 'critical caveat' in Section 5.1 is good but needs stronger language about the implication. Estimated effort: 20 minutes.
